content:
type: DEFER
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
spec: COMPRESSION FEATURE SPEC — implement in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py alongside your TTL work:

1) Cache.__init__ (dspy/clients/cache.py) — add three new optional params (append after memory_max_entries, all keyword-usable, defaults preserve current behavior):
   - compress: Optional[str] = None   # one of 'gzip', 'zlib', or None
   - cull_limit: Optional[int] = None # passed to FanoutCache(cull_limit=...) only if not None (diskcache default is 10)
   - eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None # passed to FanoutCache(eviction_policy=...) only if not None (diskcache default is 'least-recently-stored')
   Store  (validate it's one of {None,'gzip','zlib'}, raise ValueError otherwise). When constructing FanoutCache, only pass cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs if the caller supplied non-None values (so we don't override diskcache's own defaults when unset).

2) Magic header format: 4-byte magic b'DSPC' + 1 byte codec selector (0x00=none, 0x01=gzip, 0x02=zlib) + payload bytes. Payload is  optionally passed through /.

3) Cache.put (disk branch only, memory layer untouched — memory always stores the live Python object): when self.enable_disk_cache and self.compress is set (not None), serialize value as: ; if compress=='gzip': , codec_byte=0x01; if compress=='zlib': , codec_byte=0x02; then . If self.compress is None, keep existing behavior (, letting diskcache pickle it natively) — this preserves compatibility with legacy uncompressed entries written the old way.

4) Cache.get (disk branch only): when reading , if  and : read codec byte at index 4; payload = response[5:]; if codec==0x01: payload = gzip.decompress(payload); elif codec==0x02: payload = zlib.decompress(payload); elif codec==0x00: payload unchanged; then . If the bytes do NOT start with b'DSPC' (legacy raw pickle or plain non-bytes object diskcache already deserialized), use  as-is unchanged (do not attempt to reprocess) — this is the backward-compat path for pre-feature entries. This check must not raise/break for normal non-bytes cached objects (dicts, custom classes, etc.) — only branch into decompression when the value is a bytes-like object whose first 4 bytes equal the magic string.

5) Optional but requested:  method — validates and updates  for future  calls (does not retroactively rewrite existing entries). Also expose as  (works automatically once the method exists on the Cache instance since  is a Cache instance).

6) configure_cache() (dspy/clients/__init__.py) — add params: , , , forward them into the  constructor call. Update docstring Args accordingly. Also apply the same new params to the module-level  default instantiation only if you deem it necessary — defaults (None) are fine there, no behavior change needed.

7) Requirements to satisfy: round-trip integrity for gzip and zlib (put then get returns identical object incl. type); legacy header-less disk entries still load fine; disk footprint measurably smaller for large repetitive values when compression enabled; get() must return the original object type after decompression; configure_cache() must accept and apply compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy.

8) Python 3.8+ compatible typing (Optional[...], no PEP604  unions).

I will NOT touch dspy/clients/cache.py or dspy/clients/__init__.py — this is now fully yours to implement (both TTL and compression). Let me know if anything is ambiguous.

content:
type: DEFER
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
spec: COMPRESSION FEATURE SPEC - implement in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py alongside your TTL work:

1) Cache.__init__ (dspy/clients/cache.py) - add three new optional params (append after memory_max_entries, all keyword-usable, defaults preserve current behavior):
   - compress: Optional[str] = None   # one of 'gzip', 'zlib', or None
   - cull_limit: Optional[int] = None # passed to FanoutCache(cull_limit=...) only if not None (diskcache default is 10)
   - eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None # passed to FanoutCache(eviction_policy=...) only if not None (diskcache default is 'least-recently-stored')
   Store self.compress = compress (validate it's one of {None,'gzip','zlib'}, raise ValueError otherwise). When constructing FanoutCache, only pass cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs if the caller supplied non-None values (so we don't override diskcache's own defaults when unset).

2) Magic header format: 4-byte magic b'DSPC' + 1 byte codec selector (0x00=none, 0x01=gzip, 0x02=zlib) + payload bytes. Payload is cloudpickle.dumps(value) optionally passed through gzip.compress / zlib.compress.

3) Cache.put (disk branch only, memory layer untouched - memory always stores the live Python object): when self.enable_disk_cache and self.compress is set (not None), serialize value as: pickled = cloudpickle.dumps(value); if compress=='gzip': payload = gzip.compress(pickled), codec_byte=0x01; if compress=='zlib': payload = zlib.compress(pickled), codec_byte=0x02; then self.disk_cache[key] = b'DSPC' + bytes([codec_byte]) + payload. If self.compress is None, keep existing behavior (self.disk_cache[key] = value, letting diskcache pickle it natively) - this preserves compatibility with legacy uncompressed entries written the old way.

4) Cache.get (disk branch only): when reading response = self.disk_cache[key], if isinstance(response, (bytes, bytearray)) and response[:4] == b'DSPC': read codec byte at index 4; payload = response[5:]; if codec==0x01: payload = gzip.decompress(payload); elif codec==0x02: payload = zlib.decompress(payload); elif codec==0x00: payload unchanged; then response = cloudpickle.loads(payload). If the bytes do NOT start with b'DSPC' (legacy raw pickle or plain non-bytes object diskcache already deserialized), use response as-is unchanged (do not attempt to reprocess) - this is the backward-compat path for pre-feature entries. This check must not raise/break for normal non-bytes cached objects (dicts, custom classes, etc.) - only branch into decompression when the value is a bytes-like object whose first 4 bytes equal the magic string.

5) Optional but requested: Cache.set_compression(self, codec: Optional[str]) method - validates and updates self.compress for future put calls (does not retroactively rewrite existing entries). Also expose as dspy.cache.set_compression(codec) (works automatically once the method exists on the Cache instance since dspy.cache is a Cache instance).

6) configure_cache() (dspy/clients/__init__.py) - add params: compress: Optional[str] = None, cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None, forward them into the Cache(...) constructor call. Update docstring Args accordingly. Also apply the same new params to the module-level DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) default instantiation only if you deem it necessary - defaults (None) are fine there, no behavior change needed.

7) Requirements to satisfy: round-trip integrity for gzip and zlib (put then get returns identical object incl. type); legacy header-less disk entries still load fine; disk footprint measurably smaller for large repetitive values when compression enabled; get() must return the original object type after decompression; configure_cache() must accept and apply compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy.

8) Python 3.8+ compatible typing (Optional[...], no PEP604 | unions).

I will NOT touch dspy/clients/cache.py or dspy/clients/__init__.py - this is now fully yours to implement (both TTL and compression). Let me know if anything is ambiguous.
